description: Custom deal room pages at /for/[company] combining hand-crafted prospect narratives with shared dynamic components. Dark terminal aesthetic, PostHog tracking, 30-day expiry, noindex privacy. First page: /for/rime.

userStories.title: Shared Case Study Mapping
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a case study metadata mapping so prospect pages can reference case studies by slug.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/case-studies.ts with a CASE_STUDIES const object mapping slug to { title, description, tags, url }

Include all 11 published case studies: red-teaming-moderation, video-quality-at-scale, video-content-classification, prompt-enhancement-benchmark, object-identity-persistence, workplace-egocentric-data, video-model-evaluation, generative-ai-safety, fashion-ai-annotation, game-based-data-capture, egocentric-video-collection
Tags include relevant keywords (video, annotation, robotics, safety, gaming, speech, content-moderation, manufacturing, fashion, egocentric)
Export getCaseStudy(slug) helper and getCaseStudiesByTag(tag) helper
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Case studies are static Next.js pages at /case-studies/[slug]. No Supabase table. This mapping provides metadata for the CaseStudyCard component. Check the live site at claru.ai/case-studies for titles and descriptions. | COMPLETED by background agent

userStories.title: CaseStudyCard Component
userStories.description: As a prospect viewing the page, I want to see relevant case studies as rich cards.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/components/prospect/CaseStudyCard.tsx as a client component ('use client')
Props: slug: string (looks up from CASE_STUDIES mapping in src/lib/case-studies.ts)
Displays: title, description (line-clamp-2), tags as pills, 'Read Case Study →' link
Dark card style: bg #121210, border #2a2a28, accent #92B090, text #FFFFFF
Framer Motion fade-in animation on scroll using whileInView
Case study links open in new tab with rel='noopener noreferrer'
Gracefully render 'Unavailable' state when slug not found — do NOT throw
Responsive: full-width on mobile, side-by-side grid on desktop
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 for the CASE_STUDIES mapping. Design should match the landing page card style from src/app/components/sections/. Use JetBrains Mono for tag pills. | COMPLETED by background agent

userStories.title: DatasetShowcase Component
userStories.description: As a prospect, I want to see relevant datasets from Claru's catalog.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/components/prospect/DatasetShowcase.tsx as an async server component
Props: datasetIds: string[] (UUIDs from Supabase datasets table)
Fetch dataset metadata using createClient with anon key (NOT createSupabaseServerClient — same pattern as src/app/api/public/datasets/route.ts)
Query MUST filter is_published = true
Each card shows: name, category badge, source_type badge (COLLECTED/SYNTHETIC/CURATED), sample count, duration if applicable
Handle empty datasetIds array — hide section entirely
If any dataset ID returns null or is filtered by RLS, show 'Dataset unavailable' pill rather than crashing
Dark card style matching landing page: bg #121210, border #2a2a28, accent #92B090
Responsive: horizontal scroll on mobile, grid on desktop
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: IMPORTANT: Use createClient from @supabase/supabase-js with NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY directly. Do NOT use createSupabaseServerClient which passes auth cookies and causes RLS issues (this was a bug we fixed in the public datasets API). Wrap in cache() to avoid new connections per request. Dataset type is defined in src/types/data-catalog.ts. | COMPLETED by background agent

userStories.title: WorkforceStats Component
userStories.description: As a prospect, I want to see Claru's operational scale.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/components/prospect/WorkforceStats.tsx as a client component ('use client')
Display 4 stats in terminal-styled grid: Annotators (2,000+), Countries (14), Annotations Delivered (3M+), QA Turnaround (Same-day)
Stats are hardcoded constants in a WORKFORCE_STATS array — easy to update in one place
Animated counter effect on scroll using Framer Motion useInView + animate
JetBrains Mono font for numbers, Geist Sans for labels
Dark terminal aesthetic: bg #0a0908, accent #92B090 for numbers, #FFFFFF for labels
Responsive: 2x2 grid on mobile, 4-column on desktop
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Check src/app/components/sections/CatalogPreview.tsx for the existing animated counter pattern — reuse that approach. Stats should feel like a terminal readout. | COMPLETED by background agent

userStories.title: CalendlyEmbed Component
userStories.description: As a prospect, I want to book a call directly from the page.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/components/prospect/CalendlyEmbed.tsx as a client component ('use client')
Load Calendly inline embed script dynamically — only once per page
Use a hardcoded booking URL (check Supabase settings table for current booking_url value and hardcode it)
If booking_url is misconfigured, render fallback CTA button with mailto:team@claru.ai
Inline widget (not popup) with dark theme (background_color=0a0908, text_color=ffffff, primary_color=92B090)
Section header: 'Book a Call' in JetBrains Mono
Responsive: full-width embed, min-height 650px
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Calendly inline embed uses their embed.js script. Load it once in useEffect. The existing CalendlyContext in the landing page uses a popup model — this component should be self-contained with inline embed instead. Check settings table: SELECT value FROM settings WHERE key = 'booking_url'. | COMPLETED by background agent

userStories.title: ChallengesSolutions Component
userStories.description: As a prospect, I want to see my specific challenges mapped to Claru's solutions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/components/prospect/ChallengesSolutions.tsx as a client component ('use client')
Props: items: Array<{ challenge: string; solution: string }>
Two-column layout: left = 'Your Challenge' (monospace, #e8e8e8), right = 'How We Help' (accent #92B090)
Subtle connecting arrow or line between challenge and solution columns
Framer Motion stagger animation on scroll — each row animates in sequence
Section header: 'Your Challenges → Our Solutions' in JetBrains Mono
Responsive: stacks vertically on mobile (challenge above solution per item)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the most custom section per prospect page — the items array is different for every company. Keep the component generic so it just renders whatever items are passed in. | COMPLETED by background agent

userStories.title: ExpiryGate Component (30-day expiry)
userStories.description: As the business owner, I want prospect pages to auto-expire after 30 days.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/components/prospect/ExpiryGate.tsx as a client component ('use client')
Props: createdAt: string (ISO date), children: React.ReactNode
Compare current date to createdAt + 30 days
If expired: render graceful expired state with Claru branding — 'This page is no longer available. Please contact us at team@claru.ai for updated information.'
If not expired: render children normally
Show subtle 'This page expires in X days' note when < 7 days remain
Expired state uses same dark theme, centered layout, Claru logo
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Client-side date check is the primary mechanism. Server-side ISR revalidation (revalidate: 86400) in the layout provides a backup so expired pages re-render server-side daily. | COMPLETED by background agent

userStories.title: ProspectPageLayout — layout.tsx for /for/*
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a shared layout for all prospect pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/for/layout.tsx as the layout for all /for/* routes
Export metadata with robots: { index: false, follow: false }
Set X-Robots-Tag: noindex, nofollow response header as backup
Apply dark theme matching main landing page (NOT portal theme)
Minimal header: Claru logo (links to claru.ai), no navigation links
Minimal footer: 'Prepared for [Company]' text + legal links (Privacy, Terms)
Footer includes subtle 'Shared with [Name] · [Company]' watermark
Layout accepts children and passes through companyName and recipientName props via React context or slot pattern
Set revalidate: 86400 for ISR (server-side expiry backup)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: IMPORTANT: Check src/middleware.ts and ensure /for/* routes are NOT caught by portal auth middleware. If middleware checks portal paths, add an exclusion for /for/. The layout should NOT import posthog-js directly — tracking goes in a client wrapper component. Use the existing design system from src/app/globals.css. | COMPLETED by background agent

userStories.title: Privacy & SEO — robots.txt update
userStories.description: As the business owner, I want prospect pages excluded from search engines.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Disallow: /for/ to robots.txt (check if public/robots.txt exists, create if not)
Verify /for/ paths are NOT in any sitemap (check for sitemap generation in next-sitemap or similar)
Confirm no internal links from public pages point to /for/* routes (grep codebase)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The noindex meta tag is handled in US-008 layout. This story handles robots.txt and sitemap exclusion as defense-in-depth. | COMPLETED by background agent

userStories.title: PostHog Tracking Hook for Prospect Pages
userStories.description: As the sales team, I want to track prospect engagement on their pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/components/prospect/ProspectTracking.tsx as a client component ('use client')
Props: companySlug: string, companyName: string
Fire prospect_page_viewed event on mount with { company_slug, company_name, utm_source: 'prospect_page' }
Track prospect_page_scroll_depth at 25%, 50%, 75%, 100% thresholds — throttled to once per 3s
Track prospect_section_viewed via IntersectionObserver when each section enters viewport with { company_slug, section_name }
Track prospect_page_time_spent on page visibility change / unload with { company_slug, seconds_spent }
Track cta_clicked for Calendly or mailto clicks with { company_slug, cta_type }
Track case_study_clicked with { company_slug, case_study_slug }
All PostHog events include utm_source: 'prospect_page' property
PostHog logic must NOT be imported in server components
Use existing posthog-js setup (check for PostHogProvider in root layout or posthog init)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Check if posthog-js is already installed and initialized. If not, install it and add a basic init. The section tracking needs data-section attributes on each section wrapper in the prospect page. Export a useProspectTracking hook and a SectionTracker wrapper component. | COMPLETED by background agent

userStories.title: Supabase Schema — custom_page_slug on leads
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to track which leads have custom prospect pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Add custom_page_slug column (text, nullable, unique) to leads table via Supabase migration using mcp__supabase-db__apply_migration
Update Lead interface in src/types/data-catalog.ts to include custom_page_slug: string | null
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use the Supabase MCP tool: mcp__supabase-db__apply_migration. Migration SQL: ALTER TABLE leads ADD COLUMN custom_page_slug text UNIQUE; No RLS changes needed. | COMPLETED by background agent

userStories.title: /for/rime — Rime prospect page content and copy
userStories.description: As Lily at Rime, I want to see a personalized page showing Claru understands my annotation challenges.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/for/rime/page.tsx as a static page
Set PAGE_CREATED constant to today's date for expiry tracking
Wrap content in ExpiryGate with createdAt={PAGE_CREATED}
Include ProspectTracking component with companySlug='rime' companyName='Rime'
Hero section: 'Prepared for Rime' with brief context about TTS/speech AI and multilingual annotation needs

ChallengesSolutions with 3 items: (1) Hiring annotation experts across 10+ languages → Managed annotator workforce with native speakers, (2) Building annotation guidelines per language from scratch → Proven annotation protocols with linguistic QA, (3) Quality control across diverse annotators → Same-day QA pipeline with multi-reviewer consensus
2-3 CaseStudyCards using slugs: video-quality-at-scale, video-content-classification (closest proxies for annotation-at-scale)
DatasetShowcase — select 2-3 relevant published dataset IDs (query Supabase to find annotation-heavy datasets)
WorkforceStats section
CalendlyEmbed at bottom
Pass companyName='Rime' and recipientName='Lily' to layout context for footer personalization
Page matches dark terminal aesthetic with Framer Motion animations
Responsive across desktop, tablet, mobile
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the template page — future prospect pages will be created by copying this file and changing the custom content. Section order: Hero → Challenges/Solutions → Case Studies → Datasets → Workforce Stats → Book a Call. Design system: bg #0a0908, accent #92B090, text #FFFFFF, fonts Geist Sans + JetBrains Mono. Check src/app/globals.css for existing CSS variables and classes. | COMPLETED by background agent

userStories.title: Visual QA — verify /for/rime in browser
userStories.description: As QA, I need to verify the Rime prospect page renders correctly across viewports.
acceptanceCriteria: Run dev server on port 3000 or 3005
Navigate to /for/rime in browser
Take screenshots at: hero, challenges/solutions, case studies, datasets, workforce stats, calendly
Verify all 6 sections render with content (no blank sections)
Verify noindex meta tag is present in page source
Verify dark theme is applied (not portal theme or light theme)
Verify responsive at desktop (1440px) and mobile (375px)
Verify case study links open in new tab
Verify no console errors
Fix any critical visual issues found
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Playwright MCP or agent-browser skill to automate screenshots. If dev server isn't running, start it with npm run dev -- --port 3005. Check that middleware doesn't redirect /for/rime to login. | COMPLETED - all sections render, noindex present, responsive works, zero console errors
